subtitle: Audio-reactive visualizer for your browser
photosensitivityDescription:
This visualizer displays rapid flashing lights and patterns.
If you have epilepsy or are sensitive to flashing lights, viewer discretion is advised.
shareAudioDescription: Capture system or tab audio via screen sharing
localFilesDescription: Play audio files from your device
shareAudioMobileHint: Computer only - requires Chrome, Edge, or Opera

shareAudioModal.browserCompatMessage: Audio capture via screen sharing requires a Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, or Opera). You can still use
shareAudioModal.step1: and choose a screen, window, or tab to share
shareAudioModal.step2: – this is required for the visualizer to react to sound

osTips.windowsChromeOS: All sharing modes (screen, window, and tab) support audio on {{os}}. Sharing your entire screen or a window gives the cleanest experience (no browser banner).
osTips.macOSChromium: On macOS 14.2+, screen and window sharing support audio. Older macOS versions are limited to tab sharing.
osTips.macOSNonChromium: Screen sharing audio requires a Chromium browser (Chrome, Edge, Opera) on macOS 14.2+.
osTips.linux: On Linux, only tab sharing supports audio.
osTips.unknown: On Windows and ChromeOS, all sharing modes support audio. On macOS 14.2+, screen and window sharing support audio in Chrome. On Linux, only tab sharing supports audio.

sensitivityTips.title: Sensitivity & reactivity tips
sensitivityTips.description: Some presets are more ambient and less reactive than others. Check the Settings panel to adjust sensitivity and other parameters that feed the visualization engine.

localFilesModal.step1: – select one or more audio files from your device
localFilesModal.infoBox: Supports MP3, WAV, FLAC, OGG, AAC, and more. Use the playback controls to shuffle, repeat, seek, and manage your queue.

micModal.step2Bold: Let the sound drive the visuals
micModal.step2: – live music, ambient noise, or anything your mic picks up
micModal.infoBox: Silent mode: audio is captured but not played through your speakers.

spotify.connectedInfo: Now-playing metadata & cloud-synced MangoWave settings active. Playback controls require Premium. Share a screen, window, or tab playing Spotify for audio.
spotify.connectDescription: Connect Spotify for now-playing metadata and cloud-synced MangoWave settings. Spotify Premium also enables playback controls. You'll still need to share a screen, window, or tab playing Spotify for the visualizer to react to audio.
spotify.devInfo: If you are not the app owner, ensure they've added your name and email associated with your Spotify account to their developer app's User Management tab.

desktop.skipPresetsDesc: Press Space or N to skip to the next preset. Each one responds to your music differently.
desktop.curateDesc: Open the Presets panel to browse, favorite, or block presets. Build a collection of your favorites and let autopilot cycle through them.
desktop.tuneDesc: Open Settings to adjust the equalizer, smoothing, and rendering quality, shaping how the visuals react to your music.
desktop.shortcutsDesc: Press H to see all keyboard shortcuts, or find them in the Settings menu.

mobile.menuDesc: Tap the logo in the corner to open the radial menu. It gives you quick access to everything: presets, settings, fullscreen, and more.
mobile.navigateDesc: Use Next (▶) and Previous (◀) to skip between presets. Each one reacts to your music in its own way.
mobile.curateDesc: Favorite (★) the presets you love, block (⊘) the ones you don't. Open Presets (P) to browse and filter your collection.
mobile.autopilotDesc: Toggle Autopilot (A) to cycle through presets automatically. Open Settings (⚙) to adjust the equalizer and rendering.

landing.title: MangoWave | Audio Visualizer
landing.metaDescription: Free audio-reactive visualizer inspired by Winamp and MilkDrop. 400+ visuals — no signup, no install, no ads. Every beat, every drop. Just hit launch.
landing.taglineSub: Every beat, every drop. Just hit launch.
